summary:
The correct answer is C because brachytherapy specifically refers to delivering radiation using implanted radioactive sources directly within or nearby tumors—distinguishing it from distant (teletherapy) or systemic treatments. Clinical definitions emphasize physical insertion of seeds, wires, plaques, etc., which align precisely with Option C.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$

Brachytherapy involves inserting radioactive sources directly into or near targeted tissues, enabling precise local dosing while minimizing damage to surrounding areas. This distinguishes it clearly from external-beam radiotherapy (teletherapy) or systemic approaches involving radiopharmaceuticals.

Brachytherapy delivers radiation internally by placing radioactive sources directly within or adjacent to the tumor tissue, allowing highly concentrated doses with minimal impact on surrounding normal structures. Unlike teletherapy or systemically delivered agents, its mechanism relies fundamentally on physical implantation proximal to diseased sites.
